Q3 MSQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The following question is based on the diagram given below:
1. The rectangle represents government employees.
2. The triangle represents urban people.
3. The circle represents graduates.
4. The square represents clerks.
Which of the above statements is true?

 
Correct answer: B, A, C, D
Solution

(b)
The above cases may be considered as under:
For statement (a) to be true, the rectangle should lie inside the square. This is not true. Hence, (a) is false.
For statement (b) to be true, there should be a region common to the rectangle, circle and the
square. Such a region is 6. Hence, (b) is true.
Further, for statement (c) to be true, the rectangle should lie inside the circle. So, (c) is false.
For statement (d) to be true, square should lie wholly inside the rectangle, with no region common
to the circle. This is not true. So, (d) is false.

Q6 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Read the passage carefully and answer the following question.
Child psychology is certainly not a strong point with most Indian schools; why else would they inflict a double trauma on a student performing badly in the pre-boards by banning her from taking the board exams. Often with fatal results as evidenced by reports of student suicides in the run-up to the boards. Now the Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has stepped in and put the brakes on this discriminatory practice, ruling that no student can be barred from the Boards without prior clearance from the CBSE. This is good news for parents and students, many of whom have had to live with the threat of performance-linked debarment. While the school’s logic is that in order to attract talented students, they need to maintain their performance records at high levels. Chances are that a student faring poorly at the pre-boards will replicate at the boards is faulty. Chances are that the student will be spurred to work doubly hard. On the other hand, the threat of debarment, will almost certainly impact adversely on his/her performance. Of course, linking pre-boards to the boards is only one of the problems with our school system.
Which is the good news for parents, according to the passage?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
From the above passage, it is apparent that the good news for parents is that no students can be
barred from the Boards without prior clearance from the CBSE.

Q17 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Lasso regression cannot overcome severe multi-collinearity.
S2 : Logistic regression cannot overcome severe multi-collinearity.
Which of the above statements is correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• Lasso regression cannot overcome severe multi-collinearity. Lasso applies an L1 penalty that
can reduce or eliminate some correlated predictors, but under severe multi-collinearity, it
does not completely resolve the instability. So, S1 is correct.
• Logistic regression can overcome severe multi-collinearity. Logistic regression does not include
any penalty term by default and therefore suffers from multi-collinearity just like linear
regression. It cannot overcome the problem. So, S2 is incorrect.

Q18 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements about two-player games:
S1 : In a zero-sum two-player game, the total payoff of the two players is always zero.
S2 : In every zero-sum two-player game, one player must always win and the other must lose.
Which of the above statements is correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• A zero-sum game is defined such that one player’s gain is exactly equal to the other player’s loss. This means that if we add the payoffs of both players, the total is always zero. This property holds for all zero-sum two-player games. So, S1 is correct.
• Although the game is zero-sum, it does not require that one player must always win and the other must lose. Some zero-sum games can end in a tie, where neither player wins. So, S2 is incorrect.

Q25 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : The function prints the binary representation of n by first pushing remainders into a stack and then popping them to display digits in the correct order.
S2 : The function prints the binary representation of n in reverse order, since remainders are generated from least significant bit to most significant bit.
Which of the above statements is correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• The first while loop divides n repeatedly by 2 and pushes remainders into the stack.
• These remainders are binary digits in reverse order.
• The second loop pops from the stack, reversing them back to the correct order.
Hence, the function prints the binary representation of n.

Q27 NAT Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Find the minimum number of tables required for converting the following ER diagram into relational database?

 
NAT
Correct answer: 4
Solution

4
• R12 is one to many relationship R23 is one-one.
• R1 has multivalued attribute, so it will split into two tables.
Tables required are as follows:
(i) R12 R2
(ii) R1 (AB)
(iii)R1 (AC)
(iv)R23R3 (FG)
So, total 4 tables required.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

The second loop pops elements from the stack, which reverses them back to the correct order.

Q43 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following techniques can help prevent overfitting in a machine learning model?

Correct answer: B, D
Solution

(b, d)
• Adding more features without regularization increases model complexity. This makes the
model fit noise in the data, causing overfitting. So, option (a) is incorrect.
• Lasso (L1) and Ridge (L2) regression add penalties on large coefficients. This reduces model
complexity and prevents overfitting. So, option (b) is correct.
• Reducing the dataset size decreases the amount of information for learning. Less data usually
worsens overfitting. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• Dropout randomly ignores neurons during training in neural networks. This prevents the
network from relying too much on any feature and improves generalization. So, option (d) is
correct.

Q52 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider a Bayesian network with multiple variables. Gibbs sampling is applied to estimate the
joint distribution. Which of the following statements is correct?
S1 : Gibbs sampling converges to the true joint distribution after infinite iterations.
S2 : Gibbs sampling cannot handle discrete variables.
S3 : The method is guaranteed to produce exact samples in a finite number of steps.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• Gibbs sampling is a Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) method. As the number of iterations → ∞, the samples converge to the target joint distribution. So, S1 is correct.
• Gibbs sampling works for both discrete and continuous variables. There is no restriction on the type of variables as long as we can sample from the conditional distributions. So, S2 is incorrect.
• Gibbs sampling produces approximate samples, not exact ones, in a finite number of steps. Exact convergence requires infinite iteration. So, S3 is incorrect.

Q54 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider following 2 queries with relation schema :

 
Which of the following query results “all students who never got a grade above 4.0”?
Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Query 1 uses a correlated subquery with NOT EXISTS, implementing an anti-join to exclude students having any course with Grade > 4.0. It also includes student with no enrollment records. Since, no matching tuple exist in course.
• Query 2 performs a set difference between all student.ID S and those in course with grade > 4.0,
followed by a join with student to get complete records. This also retains student who never enrolled in any course.
So, both queries return the same result.

Q55 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, C, D
Solution

(a, c, d)
• PCA reduces dimensionality by projecting data onto principal components. This keeps most of the variance while simplifying the dataset. So, option (a) is correct.
• PCA reduces correlation among variables but does not completely eliminate multicollinearity
in all cases. So, option (b) is incorrect.
• By reducing dimensionality and removing redundant information, PCA can help decrease
overfitting in predictive models. So, option (c) is correct.
• Multicollinearity occurs when independent variables are highly correlated, which can cause
instability in model coefficient estimates. So, option (d) is correct.

Q56 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the sorted array:
[12, 18, 24, 30, 36, 42, 48, 54, 60, 66, 72]
If you are searching for the number 33 using binary search, how many comparisons are required before determining that 33 is not in  he list?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Binary search compares with the middle element.
Array length 11 middle index = 5 → (0-based) → middle element = 42
Compare 33 < 42 → go to left half [12, 18, 24, 30, 36] comparisons = 1
Left half length = 5 → middle index = 2 → element = 24
Compare 33 > 24 → go to right half [30, 36] comparisons = 2
Right half length = 2 → middle index = 0 → element = 30
Compare 33 > 30 → go to right half [36] comparisons = 3
Only one element left → element = 36
Compare 33 < 36 → go to left half [ ] comparisons = 4

Q57 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statement(s) is/are true?

Correct answer: A, C, D
Solution

(a, c, d)
Option (a) both Quick Sort and Merge Sort are both examples of divide and conquer. In Merge
Sort we do blind division and try to conquer on the small sub problems while in Quick Sort we
perform meaning division so we don’t bear the conquer cost.
Option (b) if we randomly choose a pivot the quick sort will not end up giving θ(n log n) time
complexity in every case but less no of groups will the worst case of θ(n2), for sure.
Option (c) every partition technique takes Ο(n) to choose the pivot whether its Hoare or Lomuto
analysis of Hoare vs Lomuto:https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quicksort
Option (d) given that we can find the median in Ο(1) we can always simulate Quick Sort to
perform in θ(n log n) but generally the median finding takes more time and Merge Sort out
performs the constant factor that gets added for median using the median of medians finding
and then applying the Quick Sort.
Correct answers will be option (a), (c) and (d).

Let X1, X2, X3 be pairwise uncorrelated random variables with E[Xi] = 0 and Var(Xi) = 2 for all i.
Find the value of Cov(X1 + 2X2, 3X2 – X3) is ________?

NAT
Correct answer: 12
Solution

12
